title: System, method and apparatus for the regeneration of nitrogen energy within a closed loop cryogenic system
abstract: A system for the regeneration of nitrogen energy within a closed loop cryogenic system is described. A liquid nitrogen storage is provided in fluid communication with a first flow line. A pump pumps liquid nitrogen from the liquid nitrogen storage to the first flow line. At least one cryogenic cooling loop is provided in fluid communication with the first flow line. The cryogenic cooling loop has an nitrogen intake and a nitrogen outlet with the nitrogen outlet being positioned downstream of the nitrogen intake. The cryogenic cooling loop has a heat exchanger between the nitrogen intake and the nitrogen outlet. A turbo expander used for re-cooling the nitrogen flowing through the first flow line and the at least one cryogenic cooling loop has an inlet and an outlet. The inlet is provided in fluid communication with the first flow line. The turbo expander is connected to a power source. A second flow line connects the outlet of the turbo expander to the liquid nitrogen storage.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A system for the regeneration of nitrogen energy within a closed loop cryogenic system, comprising: a liquid nitrogen storage having an inlet and an outlet; a first flow line having a first end and a second end, the first end of the first flow line being in fluid communication with the outlet of the liquid nitrogen storage; a pump for pumping liquid nitrogen from the liquid nitrogen storage to the first flow line; at least one cryogenic cooling loop, the at least one cryogenic cooling loop having a nitrogen intake in fluid communication with the first flow line and a nitrogen outlet in fluid communication with the first flow line downstream of the nitrogen intake, the cryogenic cooling loop having a heat exchanger between the nitrogen intake and the nitrogen outlet; a turbo expander having an inlet and an outlet, the inlet being in fluid communication with the second end of the first flow line, the turbo expander re-cooling nitrogen flowing through the first flow line and the at least one cryogenic cooling loop, the turbo expander being connected to a power source; and a second flow line having a first end and a second end, the first end of the second flow line being in fluid communication with the outlet of the turbo expander and the second end of the second flow line being in fluid communication with the inlet of the liquid nitrogen storage. 2. The system of claim 1 wherein a valve is positioned between the nitrogen intake and the heat exchanger for controlling the flow of nitrogen through the at least one cryogenic cooling loop. 3. The system of claims 1 or 2 wherein there is one cryogenic cooling loop. 4. The system of claims 1 or 2 wherein there is a first cryogenic cooling loop and a second cryogenic cooling loop, the nitrogen intake of the second cryogenic cooling loop being upstream of the nitrogen outlet of the first cryogenic cooling loop. 5. The system of any of claims 1 through 4 wherein a throttle valve is provided in the first flow line upstream of the nitrogen outlet of the at least one cryogenic cooling loop and downstream of the nitrogen intake of the at least one cryogenic cooling loop. 6. The system of any of claims 1 through 5 wherein a heat exchanger is provided in the first flow line downstream of the nitrogen outlet of the at least one cryogenic cooling loop for cooling or heating the nitrogen in the first flow line. 7. The system of any of claims 1 through 6 wherein the nitrogen storage has a vent for venting off excess pressure within the nitrogen storage. 8. The system of any of claims 1 through 7 wherein the power source is a natural gas generator. 9. The system of any of claims 1 through 8 further comprising a surge tank provided in the first flow line upstream of the turbo expander and downstream of the at least one cryogenic cooling loop. 10. The system of any of claims 1 through 9 wherein methane gas is added to the nitrogen. 11. The system of any of claims 1 through 10 wherein ethane gas is added to the nitrogen. 12. A method of regenerating nitrogen energy within a closed loop cryogenic system, comprising the steps of: pumping liquid nitrogen from a liquid nitrogen storage into a first flow line, the first flow line having a first end and a second end, the first end of the first flow line being in fluid communication with the liquid nitrogen storage; flowing at least a portion of the liquid nitrogen through at least one cryogenic cooling loop, the at least one cryogenic cooling loop having a nitrogen intake and a nitrogen outlet in fluid communication with the first flow line, the nitrogen intake of the cryogenic cooling loop being upstream of the nitrogen outlet of the cryogenic cooling loop, the cryogenic cooling loop having a heat exchanger between the nitrogen intake and the nitrogen outlet; flowing the nitrogen through a turbo expander, the turbo expander having an inlet and an outlet, the inlet being in fluid communication with the second end of the first flow line, the turbo 

 expander re-cooling nitrogen flowing through the first flow line and the at least one cryogenic cooling loop; and flowing the nitrogen through a second flow line, the second flow line having a first end and a second end, the first end of the second flow line being in fluid communication with the outlet of the turbo expander, the second end of the second flow line being in fluid communication with the inlet of the liquid nitrogen storage. 13. The method of claim 12 further comprising the step of passing the nitrogen through a heat exchanger, the heat exchanger being positioned on the first flow line downstream of the nitrogen outlet of the at least one cryogenic cooling loop, the heat exchanger cooling or heating the nitrogen passing through. 14. The method of claims 12 or 13 wherein there is one cryogenic cooling loop. 15. The method of claims 12 or 13 wherein there is a first cryogenic cooling loop and a second cryogenic cooling loop, the nitrogen intake of the second cryogenic cooling loop being upstream of the nitrogen outlet of the first cryogenic cooling loop. 16. The method of any of claims 12 through 15 further comprising the step of passing the nitrogen through a surge tank prior to flowing the nitrogen through the turbo expander. 17. The method of any of claims 12 through 16 wherein methane gas is added to the nitrogen. 18. The method of any of claims 12 through 17 wherein ethane gas is added to the nitrogen. 19. An apparatus for using nitrogen in a closed loop cryogenic system, comprising: a cryochamber having a first nitrogen flow line, the first nitrogen flow line having an inlet for connection to a nitrogen source and an outlet, at least one cryogenic cooling loop having a nitrogen inlet and a nitrogen outlet, the nitrogen inlet and the nitrogen outlet being in fluid communication with the first nitrogen flow line, the nitrogen inlet being upstream of the nitrogen outlet, the at least one cryogenic cooling loop having a heat exchanger through which the nitrogen passes, the heat exchanger 

 having a fluid inlet for introducing fluid to be cooled into the heat exchanger and a fluid outlet for discharging the fluid cooled in the heat exchanger; a turbo expander, the turbo expander having an inlet in fluid communication with the outlet of the first nitrogen flow line and an outlet in fluid communication with the nitrogen source, the turbo expander re-cooling the nitrogen that passes through the first flow line and the at least one cryogenic cooling loop; and at least one storage tank in fluid communication with the fluid outlet of the heat exchanger of the at least one cryogenic cooling loop. 20. The apparatus of claim 19 wherein the cryochamber is enclosed with insulated walls. 21. The apparatus of claims 19 or 20 wherein the turbo expander is enclosed with insulated walls. 22. The apparatus of claim 20 wherein the insulated walls enclosing the cryochamber has at least one access door. 23. The apparatus of claim 21 wherein the insulated walls enclosing the turbo expander has at least one access door. 24. The apparatus of any of claims 19 through 23 wherein the cryochamber further comprises a heat exchanger positioned on the first nitrogen flow line downstream of the nitrogen outlet of the cryogenic cooling loop. 25. The apparatus of any of claims 19 through 24 wherein a flow control valve is provided on the first nitrogen flow line between the nitrogen inlet and the nitrogen outlet of the cryogenic cooling loop. 26. The apparatus of any of claims 19 through 25 wherein a flow control valve is positioned in the at least one cryogenic cooling loop adjacent to the nitrogen inlet. 27. The apparatus of any of claims 19 through 26 wherein the cryochamber, the turbo expander and the at least one storage tank are mounted on a skid. 28. The apparatus of claim 27 wherein the skid has a steel grate surface. 29. The apparatus of any of claims 19 through 28 wherein a flare line is connected to the at least one storage tank. 30. The apparatus of any of claims 19 through 29 further comprising a surge tank provided in the first flow line upstream of the turbo expander and downstream of the at least one cryogenic cooling loop.
